title: Direct Materials Purchases Budget
event: Reyes Co. plans to produce 25,000 units; each requires 2 pounds of material at $3 per pound. Desired ending raw materials are 4,000 pounds; beginning raw materials are 5,000 pounds. Compute budgeted purchases in dollars.

computationPaths.narration: Same skeleton as the production budget, one level down the supply chain — needs plus ending stock minus what's on hand.

memorize.body: Purchases (lbs) = production needs + desired ending RM − beginning RM; then × cost per pound.
The budgets chain: sales → production → materials. Each feeds the next, each uses the same +ending −beginning pattern.

questions.prompt: Needs 50,000 lbs, ending RM 4,000, beginning RM 5,000, $3/lb. Budgeted purchases = ?

distractors.feedback: You priced production needs (50,000 lbs) and ignored the inventory adjustment — purchases are 49,000 lbs.
RM signs flipped (50 + 5 − 4). Add the ENDING target, subtract the BEGINNING stock: 49,000 lbs × $3.
